exports.name: registerDevLink

packages/cli/src/commands/hub.ts.summary: haive hub — shared team-knowledge hub operations.

packages/cli/src/commands/ingest.ts.summary: CI USAGE — integrate haive ingest in your pipeline: # ESLint / any SARIF emitter eslint --format @microsoft/eslint-formatter-sarif --output-file eslint.sarif src/ haive ingest --from sarif eslint.sarif --scope team --min-severity major # SonarQube file export haive ingest --from sonar sonar-issues.json --scope team --min-severity major # SonarQube live API (no file needed — Node 18+) haive ingest --from sonar-api \ --sonar-url "$SONAR_HOST_URL" --sonar-token "$SONAR_TOKEN" \ --sonar-component my_project --min-severity major # Dry-run to preview without writing haive ingest --from sarif report.sarif --dry-run Exit codes: 0 = success (even when nothing new), 1 = bad args or unreadable report.

exports.name: isExpectedNonzeroExit
exports.description: Commands that routinely exit non-zero WITHOUT a real failure, so a bare exit code is not a failure signal for them: `grep`/`rg` (exit 1 = no match), `find`, `test`/`[`/`[[`, `diff` (exit 1 = differs), and ANY pipeline (the last stage — often `head` — sets the exit code, and SIGPIPE/`head` closing a pipe makes earlier stages "fail").

exports.name: detectFailure
exports.description: Detect whether the tool response signals a failure worth capturing as mem_tried.

packages/core/src/config.ts.summary: hAIve project configuration — .ai/haive.config.json In autopilot mode, hAIve operates with zero human intervention: - Memories go directly to `validated` (no approval cycle) - `haive sync` auto-approves proposed memories after the delay - The MCP server saves a session recap automatically on exit - `get_briefing` auto-generates a minimal project context if none exists - `haive sync` applies safe self-maintenance repairs (context version, headings, needs_anchor tags, code-map refresh) without human intervention Multi-repo support: - crossRepoSources: pull shared memories from other repos on haive sync - contractFiles: watch API contract files for breaking changes - hubPath: local path to a shared team-knowledge hub repo

exports.name: CrossRepoSource
exports.description: A remote or local repo to pull shared memories from.

exports.name: ContractFile
exports.description: An API contract file to snapshot and monitor for breaking changes.

exports.name: AntiPatternGate
exports.description: The pre-commit anti-pattern gate hardness levels.

exports.name: antiPatternGateParams
exports.description: Single source of truth mapping a configured `antiPatternGate` to the `pre_commit_check` parameters that implement it.

exports.name: findTopicStatusConflictPairs
exports.description: Same `topic` key with opposed trust (validated vs rejected) — advisory; use `mem_conflicts_with` per id next.

exports.name: findLexicalConflictPairs
exports.description: Bulk heuristic: lexical similarity pairs for human review → often followed by `mem_conflicts_with`.

exports.name: recordProjectContextEmission
exports.description: Record that this exact project-context body was just emitted.

exports.name: watchContracts
exports.description: Check all configured contract files for changes.

exports.name: isCovered
exports.description: True when `file` equals or is nested under any covered path prefix.

exports.name: findCoverageGaps
exports.description: Cross hot files with the coverage index → the uncovered, frequently-edited files.

packages/core/src/distinctive.ts.summary: Distinctive-token corroboration for the anti-pattern gate.

exports.name: CODE_STOPWORDS
exports.description: Language keywords + ubiquitous code words that would match almost any memory body and so carry no distinguishing signal.

exports.name: MIN_WORD_LEN
exports.description: Minimum token length kept for word-level matching (shorter tokens are too noisy).

exports.name: tokenizeWords
exports.description: Split text into lowercase word tokens (>= MIN_WORD_LEN, excluding code stopwords).

exports.name: buildDocFrequency
exports.description: Build per-token document frequency across a corpus of memory bodies.

exports.name: distinctiveCap
exports.description: Document-frequency cap at/below which a token counts as distinctive.

exports.name: isDistinctiveToken
exports.description: True when `token` is distinctive (rare) within the corpus.

exports.name: diffHasDistinctiveOverlap
exports.description: True when the added diff text shares at least one *distinctive* word token with the memory body.

exports.name: computeEvalTrend
exports.description: Pure trend over the history (chronological order is enforced internally).

exports.name: RetrievalCase

exports.name: scoreRetrievalCase
exports.description: Score one retrieval case from the (ranked) ids the briefing surfaced.

exports.name: overallScore
exports.description: Combine retrieval + sensor aggregates into a single 0..100 quality score.

exports.name: MetricDelta
exports.description: Baseline / compare — makes the "hAIve improves retrieval by N%" claim reproducible.

exports.name: compareEvalReports
exports.description: Diff a current report against a baseline.

exports.name: titleFromBody
exports.description: Extract a short task-like title from a memory body (first heading or first line).

exports.name: synthesizeSelfEvalCases
exports.description: Synthesize retrieval cases from a repo's own anchored memories — zero-setup eval.

exports.name: findUncapturedFailures

exports.description: Normalize a tool-specific severity string to the shared scale.

exports.description: Parse SARIF 2.1.0 (`runs[].results[]`).

packages/core/src/gate-precision.ts.summary: Gate signal-quality — is the inferential (anti-pattern) gate earning trust or crying wolf? hAIve's anti-pattern gate is probabilistic and warmup-sensitive, so it is deliberately calibrated NOT to hard-block on weak matches.

exports.name: computeGatePrecision
exports.description: Compute the gate's signal quality from prevention events + usage.

exports.description: Suggest loosening the gate when it is noisy (low precision with real rejection volume), or tightening it when it is precise but currently soft.

exports.name: compareGatePrecision

exports.name: STACK_PACK_TAG

exports.description: Best-effort inference: given a list of file paths, infer module names from conventional layouts (packages/X/, apps/X/, modules/X/, src/X/).

exports.name: readRuntimeJournalTail
exports.description: Read last N valid JSON lines (oldest-first in returned array).

exports.name: SensorSchema

exports.name: suggestSensorFromMemory

exports.description: Run every memory's regex sensor against every applicable target.

exports.description: A shell/test sensor selected for execution — the feedback *computational* layer that a regex can't express.

exports.description: Progressive disclosure for `skill` memories.

exports.name: evaluateSkillActivation
exports.description: Decide whether a skill should be disclosed for the given context.

exports.name: isSkillSuppressed
exports.description: Convenience: true when a skill defines activation triggers that the context does NOT satisfy.

exports.description: Estimate how unguessable / team-specific a memory body is, in [0, 1].

exports.name: CHARS_PER_TOKEN

exports.description: Allocate a global token budget across N parts with relative weights, then truncate each part to its share.

exports.description: Parse a since string: ISO date, or relative like '7d', '24h', '30m'.

exports.name: memConflicts
